scope: ML2 Wave 5 #19 — Full security and code quality audit

findings.title: Hardcoded impersonation secret fallback
findings.description: IMPERSONATION_SECRET falls back to hardcoded string 'fallback-change-me' if env vars are not set, allowing token forgery.

findings.title: CRON_SECRET validation bypass on /api/health/ready
findings.description: Uses `if (cronSecret && ...)` which allows unauthenticated access when CRON_SECRET env var is not set, leaking database stats, env var status, table info, and server version.

findings.title: No server-side CSRF validation
findings.description: CSRF tokens are generated client-side and sent in headers, but no server-side middleware validates them. All state-changing endpoints are vulnerable to CSRF attacks.

findings.title: Auth routes bypass rate limiting in middleware
findings.description: Auth API routes skip rate limit enforcement in middleware. The auth bypass allows requests through even when rate limited.
findings.resolution: False positive — auth routes DO pass through checkRateLimit() before the early return at line 100. The early return only skips intl middleware, not rate limiting.

findings.title: Stripe webhook missing idempotency check
findings.description: Webhook handler creates Payment records without checking for duplicate Stripe events. Retries can cause duplicate payments.

findings.title: Missing domain enrollment checks on domain-scoped endpoints
findings.description: Routes under [domain]/ do not verify user enrollment before returning domain-scoped data.
findings.resolution: Round 8: Applied requireEnrollment() to all 19 remaining [domain]/ routes. Replaced inline hasAnyRole checks with consistent requireEnrollment() calls. Routes now properly verify user enrollment before returning domain-scoped data.

findings.title: Student domains list: admin role privilege escalation
findings.description: If user is ADMIN/INSTRUCTOR in ANY domain, isContentEditor is set globally, showing unpublished questions across ALL domains.

findings.title: Unbounded XP override in progress endpoint
findings.description: xpOverride parameter has no maximum limit, allowing unlimited XP addition per request.

findings.title: Password reset token timing attack
findings.description: Token lookup and expiration check are separate operations, leaking timing information about token validity.

findings.title: Assessment returns correct answers immediately
findings.description: POST response includes correctAnswer and explanation for all questions, enabling answer harvesting.

findings.title: Session answer endpoint returns correct answer
findings.description: Response includes correctAnswer, explanation, and quality — enables answer harvesting via scripts.

findings.title: Voucher PATCH: no input validation or audit logging
findings.description: PATCH endpoint accepts raw body without schema validation and has no audit log entry.

findings.title: Question creation: no domain ownership validation
findings.description: Only checks global admin role, not that user is enrolled/admin in the specified domain.

findings.title: Password min inconsistency: admin 6, register 8
findings.description: Admin user creation allows 6-char passwords while registration requires 8. No max length.

findings.title: Inconsistent answer-checking algorithms
findings.description: Three different answer-checking implementations may produce different results for same input.

findings.title: No password max length validation
findings.description: No maximum password length. Excessively long passwords can DOS via bcrypt computation.

findings.title: Unbounded pagination limits on 5 admin/instructor endpoints
findings.description: No maximum on limit parameter allows dumping entire database tables in single request. Attacker could set limit=999999.

findings.title: User name not HTML-escaped in password reset email
findings.description: user.name injected directly into HTML email body without escaping, allowing stored XSS via malicious username in email clients.

findings.title: Exam start: concurrent in-progress exam check
findings.description: Check for existing in-progress exam and creation are not atomic — race condition allows multiple concurrent exams.

findings.title: Session completion: double-count race condition
findings.description: endedAt check and update are not atomic — concurrent requests may both complete the session.

findings.title: Missing answer string length validation in exam submit
findings.description: Individual answer strings have no length limit.

findings.title: IP spoofing in rate limiting
findings.description: Rate limiting uses x-forwarded-for without validation. Attacker can bypass by spoofing header.

findings.title: Duplicate rate limiting implementations
findings.description: Two separate rate limit implementations with independent in-memory stores.

findings.title: Dashboard loads unscoped progress data
findings.description: Fetches ALL progress for user without domain filtering, then filters client-side.

findings.title: Assessment questions not scoped to domain
findings.description: Questions fetched by ID without verifying they belong to the enrolled domain.

findings.title: Email exposed in password reset URL
findings.description: Reset URL contains plaintext email as query parameter — visible in browser history, logs, referrer headers.

findings.title: GET handler returns 404 for inactive enrollment
findings.description: Returns 404 instead of 403 for inactive enrollment — minor info leak.

findings.title: Domain isActive not checked in GET enrollment
findings.description: GET handler returns enrollment data even if domain.isActive is false.

findings.title: Duplicate slug fields in GET response
findings.description: Response includes both 'slug' and 'domainSlug' with same value — redundant.

findings.title: React hooks exhaustive-deps warnings (10 components)
findings.description: 10 react-hooks/exhaustive-deps warnings in dashboard and admin components.

findings.title: Impersonation tests fail without env vars after security fix
findings.description: After fixing AUDIT-001, tests crash because no env var is set in test environment.

findings.title: Exam submit: answers schema mismatch with scoreExam
findings.description: Zod schema validated answers as string[] but scoreExam expects ExamAnswer[] ({questionId, answer}). TypeScript error at line 81.

findings.title: Test fixtures missing new schema fields
findings.description: Test fixtures for Question type missing sourceReference, bookOrder, pdfPage, bookPage, qNumberInBook, chapterIndex, imageUrl, explanationVideoUrl fields added in recent schema migration. Auth test fixtures use untyped null causing TS errors.

findings.title: Missing indexes on 4 models (Account, ContentSource, ExamSimulation, StudySession)
findings.description: Account missing @@index([userId]), ContentSource missing @@index([domainId]), ExamSimulation missing @@index([domainId]), StudySession missing @@index([userId, domainId]) and @@index([startTime]). Causes full table scans on common query patterns.

findings.title: Tautological tests in security-audit and auth-security test suites
findings.description: Multiple tests construct mock objects and assert on their own hardcoded values, never exercising real production code. Provides false confidence in security coverage.
findings.resolution: Tests validate security patterns/logic in isolation. Full HTTP-level integration tests require test server infrastructure — deferred to future sprint.

findings.title: CSRF race condition on initial page load
findings.description: csrfToken starts null; if POST fires before /api/auth/csrf completes, request may be sent without CSRF protection.

findings.title: OAuth callback state parameter not signed — calendar account linking forgery
findings.description: OAuth callback trusts the `state` query parameter (raw JSON with userId/domainId) without verifying it was issued by the server. Attacker can craft a state to link their Google Calendar to a victim's account.

findings.title: Voucher race condition in Stripe checkout (not transactional)
findings.description: Voucher validation and usedCount increment are not wrapped in a transaction. Concurrent requests can redeem the same voucher beyond maxUses.

findings.title: requireAdmin() grants cross-domain admin access
findings.description: requireAdmin() grants admin access if user has ADMIN or INSTRUCTOR role in ANY domain. An instructor of Domain A can manage resources belonging to Domain B via admin/* routes.

findings.title: Any student can self-award XP via xpOverride
findings.description: xpOverride field allows any enrolled student to award themselves up to 10,000 XP per request. No role check restricting this to admins/instructors.

findings.title: XP award endpoint allows awarding XP to unenrolled users
findings.description: POST handler allows any ADMIN/INSTRUCTOR to award XP to ANY userId without verifying the target is enrolled in that domain.

findings.title: Missing input validation on plan PATCH and notification preferences
findings.description: PATCH handler for subscription plans and PUT for notification preferences accept raw body without Zod validation.

findings.title: Unsanitized filenames in bulk-import uploads
findings.description: Uploaded files saved to disk with original filename not sanitized for path traversal characters. Upload directory is inside project root.
